children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: When the Secretary determines that sufficient land has been acquired to constitute an area that can be efficiently managed as a National Wildlife Refuge, the Secretary shall establish the Baca National Wildlife Refuge, as generally depicted on the map.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: B Effective date
children.text: The establishment of the refuge under subparagraph (A) shall be effective upon publication of a notice of the Secretary’s determination in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the Baca National Wildlife Refuge shall be to restore, enhance, and maintain wetland, upland, riparian, and other habitats for native wildlife, plant, and fish species in the San Luis Valley.

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: b Availability of map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall administer all lands and interests therein acquired within the boundaries of the national wildlife refuge in accordance with the National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966 ( et seq.) and the Act of ( et seq.) (commonly known as the Refuge Recreation Act).16 U.S.C. 668dd16 U.S.C. 460kSeptember 28, 1962

children.label: B
children.text: take into consideration the role of the Refuge in broader landscape conservation efforts.
protect and maintain irrigation water rights necessary for the protection of monument, park, preserve, and refuge resources and uses;
minimize, to the extent consistent with the protection of national wildlife refuge resources, adverse impacts on other water users; and

subject to any agreement in existence as of , and to the extent consistent with the purposes of the Refuge, use decreed water rights on the Refuge in approximately the same manner that the water rights have been used historically.March 11, 2009
